name: cover-kicker
text: HOOK

name: cover-title
text: Native objects survive the handoff

name: cover-subtitle
text: This deck proves that text, shapes, data, and notes remain editable after export.
textPreview: This deck proves that text, shapes, data, and notes remain editable after export.

name: cover-proof
text:
REAL TEXT
REAL SHAPES
REAL DATA
textPreview: REAL TEXT | REAL SHAPES | REAL DATA

name: cover-meta
text: Editorial Technical Minimal
Frame editability as a testable delivery contract, not a marketing adjective.

name: slide-2-eyebrow
text: CONTRACT

name: slide-2-action-title
text: Editability requires four independent proofs

name: slide-2-message
text: A valid native handoff proves object structure, rendered fidelity, clean copy, and documented exceptions.
textPreview: A valid native handoff proves object structure, rendered fidelity, clean copy, and documented exceptions.

name: slide-2-items
text:
1. Object inspection
2. Rendered QA
3. Text extraction
4. Exception list
textPreview: 1. Object inspection | 2. Rendered QA | 3. Text extraction | 4. Exception list

text: Explain that any one missing proof leaves an editability claim incomplete.

name: slide-3-eyebrow
text: PROOF

name: slide-3-action-title
text: Native charts keep the underlying data changeable
textPreview: Native charts keep the underlying data changeable

name: slide-3-message
text: The chart is a PowerPoint chart object with editable categories and series values.
textPreview: The chart is a PowerPoint chart object with editable categories and series values.

name: slide-3-evidence
text: Illustrative fixture values created for this benchmark
textPreview: Illustrative fixture values created for this benchmark

text: Open the chart data in PowerPoint and change any value to verify the promise.

name: slide-4-eyebrow
text: EVIDENCE

name: slide-4-action-title
text: Native tables make the handoff auditable
textPreview: Native tables make the handoff auditable

name: slide-4-message
text: Each promise is paired with a concrete artifact and a pass signal.
textPreview: Each promise is paired with a concrete artifact and a pass signal.

text: Use the matrix as the release checklist for future native cases.

name: slide-5-eyebrow
text: GATE

name: slide-5-action-title
text: The release gate catches flattened impostors
textPreview: The release gate catches flattened impostors

name: slide-5-step-1-title
text: Plan

name: slide-5-step-1-detail
text: Validate the story and native payload.
textPreview: Validate the story and native payload.

name: slide-5-step-2-title
text: Build

name: slide-5-step-2-detail
text: Create real PowerPoint objects.

name: slide-5-step-3-title
text: Reopen

name: slide-5-step-3-detail
text: Import the exported PPTX again.

name: slide-5-step-4-title
text: Render

name: slide-5-step-4-detail
text: Inspect every final slide.

name: slide-5-step-5-title
text: Inspect

name: slide-5-step-5-detail
text: Block image-only or missing-object output.
Stress that reopening the exported file tests the deliverable rather than the in-memory draft.

name: closing-kicker
text: HANDOFF

name: closing-title
text: Every editable promise now leaves evidence
textPreview: Every editable promise now leaves evidence

name: closing-message
text: The deck, previews, inspection log, editability report, and QA record travel together.
textPreview: The deck, previews, inspection log, editability report, and QA record travel together.

name: closing-meta
text: Native objects survive the handoff
Close by making the evidence package the reusable definition of done.
